importance.title: Why does it matter?
importance.subtitle: The digital environment poses invisible risks that impact young people's learning and well-being. We act at the root cause.

items.title: Disinformation & Fake News
items.desc: The proliferation of false stories undermines public trust and misleads young people. We teach them to read beyond the click.

items.title: Digital Radicalization
items.desc: Algorithms create bubbles that can expose teenagers to hate speech and extremist views. We build cognitive resilience to these manipulations.

items.title: Critical Thinking
items.desc: More than just using digital devices, young people must learn to question sources, motivations, and the context of what they consume online.

items.title: Digital Safety
items.desc: We empower the next generation to browse autonomously, protecting their privacy and fostering healthy relationships online.

solutions.title: Our Solutions
solutions.subtitle: A unique combination of serious games, cognitive psychology, and student-centered design.

meiasVerdades.title: Meias#Verdades
meiasVerdades.desc: A revolutionary educational mobile game based on Inoculation Theory (prebunking). Young people learn disinformation techniques by playing as content creators, building 'mental antibodies' against online manipulation.

meiasVerdadesMemory.title: Meias#Verdades Memory Game
meiasVerdadesMemory.desc: A dynamic physical (offline) card game designed for classrooms and group activities. It encourages face-to-face discussions, stimulates cooperation, and introduces essential digital literacy concepts.

codigoPata.title: Código P.A.T.A.
codigoPata.desc: Our next digital platform under development. A complete gamified ecosystem for schools that expands computational and media literacy skills for middle school students.

methodology.title: The Science behind the Methodology
methodology.subtitle: We do not rely on guesswork. Our learning ecosystem is built upon consolidated scientific pillars.

cards.title: Serious Games
cards.desc: Playful mechanics converted into learning retention.

cards.title: Microlearning
cards.desc: High-absorption fractional lessons for youth audiences.

items.title: Serious Games
items.desc: Game-based learning triggers deep emotional and cognitive engagement, ensuring long-term knowledge retention.

items.title: Inoculation Theory
items.desc: Just like a medical vaccine, we expose students to weakened doses of digital manipulation techniques so they can recognize and reject them in the real world.

items.title: Microlearning
items.desc: Content broken down into highly focused micro-doses, tailored to the pace and attention span of modern teens.

items.title: Information Science & Psychology
items.desc: Rigorous analysis of human informational behavior and cognitive decision-making processes in digital networks.

founder.title: Leadership & Scientific Foundation
founder.subtitle: Born in academia, designed for the world.
founder.name: Karoline Fernandes
founder.bio: Holding a PhD and a solid track record of scientific research in Media and Information Literacy, Karoline Fernandes leads Literacia Edu with a clear vision: turning cutting-edge academic research into high-impact social solutions. With international research experience at KU Leuven (Belgium) and projects supported by SUDENE, she oversees the development of the Meias#Verdades game and drives the national and international expansion of the startup.
founder.quote: "Our mission is to equip children and teens with the cognitive antibodies required to navigate the internet with independence and safety."

ctaFinal.title: Preparing citizens for the digital age
ctaFinal.subtitle: Join us in our mission to raise a more critical, secure, and resilient generation against online lies. Shall we talk?

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Transforming scientific evidence into shields against disinformation.
about.historyDesc: Literacia Edu was born as an academic research project focused on solving one of the most pressing issues of the contemporary era: the vulnerability of young generations to fake news, disinformation, and online radicalization. Led by researcher Karoline Fernandes, the initiative evolved from academic theses and lab testing to become a Brazilian EdTech with social impact. Today, we unite national and international universities to bring media literacy to public and private schools, proving that science and playful education are the best paths to a healthy digital future.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.desc: To empower children and adolescents to develop critical thinking and digital resilience, providing innovative educational tools based on scientific evidence.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.desc: To be the leading global reference in educational solutions for youth media literacy and combating disinformation by 2030, integrating technology, psychology, and science.

items.title: Scientific Rigor
items.desc: All of our methodologies and games are built on top of active, peer-reviewed, and validated scientific research.

items.title: Educational Innovation
items.desc: We create learning experiences that truly resonate with young audiences through gamification.

items.title: Ethics & Transparency
items.desc: We promote truth, data privacy, and social responsibility in every project.

items.title: Social Inclusion
items.desc: We work actively to bring our tools to both private and public school systems.

about.scienceDesc1: Our main methodological foundation lies in Psychological Inoculation Theory, popularly referred to in digital communication as Prebunking. Simply put, just as a medical vaccine introduces a weakened virus to train the immune system, our games present users, in a controlled and playful environment, with manipulation techniques (such as emotional appeals, false dilemmas, and scapegoating).
about.scienceDesc2: By learning how to create and recognize these techniques through gameplay, young people develop cognitive resistance. Thus, when they encounter real disinformation on their daily social media feeds, they instantly spot it and reject impulsive sharing.
about.vacinaDesc: Media literacy at Literacia Edu is not passive. Based on Inoculation Theory, we teach manipulation techniques before young people experience them.
about.inoculacaoDesc: Like a vaccine that stimulates internal defenses, we present fake news techniques in a simplified way to develop critical thinking antibodies.

items.title: Origins in Research
items.desc: Initiation of the conceptual development of media literacy solutions at UFPE.

items.title: International Connection
items.desc: Karoline Fernandes conducts international research at the prestigious KU Leuven in Belgium, refining the inoculation model.

items.title: Government Support
items.desc: Acceleration by the SUDENE program and field tests in public schools in Pernambuco, yielding highly positive results.

items.title: Foundation & Launch
items.desc: Formal incorporation of Literacia Edu as a commercial EdTech and expansion of the mobile and physical game portfolio.

solutions.title: Our Educational Solutions
solutions.subtitle: Intelligent gamification to develop alert and resilient minds.

cta.title: Want to see it up close at your school or institution?
cta.desc: We offer guided pilot programs and complete pedagogical consulting for education departments and school networks.

meiasVerdades.title: Meias#Verdades Mobile Game
meiasVerdades.subtitle: A cognitive vaccine against social media disinformation.
features: Social media simulator integrated within a secure environment.
Educational roleplay: students play from the malicious content creator's perspective to understand internal mechanics.
Modules based on real-world techniques: bot usage, hyper-emotional appeals, conspiracy theories, and artificial polarization.
Real-time media literacy progress metrics for educators.
meiasVerdades.audience: Recommended for middle and high schools (ages 11 to 17).

mockup.quote: "Share this lie to earn engagement points."
mockup.desc: The game challenges students to create posts with emotional appeal or scapegoating to teach the risks behind the easy click.

memoryGame.title: Meias#Verdades Memory Game (Offline)
memoryGame.subtitle: In-person, dynamic, and cooperative media literacy.
memoryGame.featuresTitle: Why use it in the classroom?
features: Perfect for schools with smartphone usage restrictions in classrooms.
Promotes teacher-mediated face-to-face dialogue, developing soft skills like cooperation and debate.
Card deck with exclusive illustrations and real problem-solving cases adapted for group discussions.
Complete teacher's guide aligned with modern curriculum competencies.
memoryGame.audience: Recommended for elementary and middle schools (ages 8 to 14).

codigoPata.title: Código P.A.T.A.
codigoPata.subtitle: The future frontier of digital literacy.
codigoPata.desc: Código P.A.T.A. is a cross-platform gamified ecosystem in late-stage development. The project expands classic media literacy to incorporate basic concepts of artificial intelligence algorithms, automated feed curation, and ethical digital citizenship. The solution will integrate digital authorship tools, allowing students to design their own digital literacy campaigns within their school.
codigoPata.status: Product under development. Release scheduled for next semester.
